raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint about American Express regarding a billing dispute that was originally resolved and then reversed nearly a year later. 

In late XXXX, I reported several unauthorized transactions totaling approximately {$33000.00} made at a merchant. American Express credited the full amount roughly 15 days after I filed the dispute, confirming that the charges were found to be unauthorized. 

On or about XX/XX/XXXX, American Express reversed that creditapproximately XXXX days after the original disputestating that the merchant provided evidence the charges were valid. This reversal appears to be outside the XXXX time limit set under the Fair Credit Billing Act for resolving billing disputes. 

Since the reversal, I have contacted American Express multiple times. Representatives on XX/XX/XXXX, told me that : - There is evidence from the merchant proving the charges were valid, but they could not share or describe that evidence. 
Two representatives escalated my case to their leadership teams and said I would receive a response within XXXX days. 
Later, I was informed that video proof exists of me signing a receipt, but Amex stated that I would need to obtain this evidence through a subpoena rather than being provided it directly. I have always acknowledge that I was physically at the merchant, and did authorize charges at check out but want to be clear that I did not authorize the additional or subsequent charges beyond the legitimate purchase. 

Also, after I contacted American Express originally about the reversal, it took the company over a week to call me back. I ultimately had to call them myself to get an update. When I finally reached a representative, they told me I would hear back within XXXX hours, no one contacted me during that timeframe. The lack of timely communication and responsiveness has made it very difficult to resolve this dispute or obtain the evidence Amex claims to have from the merchant. 

I have never received any written documentation supporting the reversal or showing that the additional transactions were authorized. I am requesting that American Express : XXXX. Provide the documentation or evidence relied upon to justify the reversal ; XXXX. Reinstate the {$33000.00} credit if the transactions can not be substantiated ; and XXXX. Provide a written explanation compliant with the Fair Credit Billing Act.
